title: Ship Rotation, Thrust, Inertia, Drag, and Speed Limits · Traceback Repair Bay
summary: A preserved game trace contains one plausible state, timing, input, vector, rendering, collision, ownership, audio, accessibility, packaging, or release defect; locate the first failed invariant before editing. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in frame-rate-independent inertial ship controller and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Update angle from action intent and derive a normalized facing vector without overwriting velocity direction.
Integrate acceleration into velocity and velocity into position with explicit seconds and bounded time.
Preserve momentum and apply a time-scaled damping model whose behavior is tested across frame rates.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Order the regression proof · Debug Ship Feel Evidence
steps.instruction:
Order the Ship Feel Evidence Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python player-input-to-release evidence cycle for this debug task before implementing it.

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Order the regression proof · Debug Ship Feel Evidence.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Ship Feel Evidence assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Order the regression proof · Debug Ship Feel Evidence, keep Ship Feel Evidence connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.text: A debug Asteroids team handles case 2548 during a keyboard-only community arcade night. Build deterministic Python evidence to integrate rotation, thrust, inertia, drag, and speed limits with frame-rate-independent Vector2 motion; preserve explicit pixels, seconds, radians or degrees, frames, entity counts, collision units, and budgets; then run the packaged build with dummy video and audio drivers, missing optional sound, and a corrupted save. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Tune rotation, acceleration, drag, cap, input latency, and feedback against recorded player tasks and objective traces.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Update angle from action intent and derive a normalized facing vector without overwriting velocity direction. Artifact under review: frame-rate-independent inertial ship controller.

options.text: The Ship Facing record says frame-rate-independent inertial ship controller looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. That claim fails the inspect the failure record evidence boundary for Ship Facing.

For inspect the failure record, run one convenient high-frame-rate happy path, omit revision, seed, input and time trace, units, seams, device changes, entity reconciliation, multichannel cues, accessibility settings, failures, recovery, and desktop transfer, then accept a playable animation as Asteroids proof. This does not satisfy Ship Facing for frame-rate-independent inertial ship controller.
The record names the assumption, applies Ship Facing, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Ship Feel Evidence
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: asteroids-ship-feel-evidence-n27123" that uses case 1598 to integrate rotation, thrust, inertia, drag, and speed limits with frame-rate-independent Vector2 motion. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not claim native pygame-ce, SDL, window, controller, mixer, filesystem, package, GPU, installer, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, released pygame-ce 2.5.7, fixed and headless SDL fixtures, real devices, licensed assets, accessibility playtests, profiling, clean installations, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Ship Feel Evidence.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Ship Feel Evidence into observable performance evidence. For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Ship Feel Evidence, keep Ship Feel Evidence connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: asteroids-ship-feel-evidence-n27123
# Competency: asteroids-ship-feel-evidence.
# Case 1598: a low-power integrated-GPU frame-budget trial.
# Operating constraint: record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, playtest observation, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: lose window focus while thrust and fire are held, then prove both actions release safely.
def evidence_asteroids_ship_feel_evidence_n27123(speed: float = 140.0, thrust: float = 80.0, drag: float = 0.15, max_speed: float = 260.0, dt: float = 0.016) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_n27123 = "n27123-32172n-1598"
    assert evidence_variant_n27123
    evidence_axes_n27123 = (
        "playern27123",
        "seed1598",
        "input32172n",
        "framen271231598",
        "state1598n27123",
        "playtest32172n1598",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_n27123)) == 6
    if min(speed, thrust, drag, max_speed, dt) < 0.0:
        return False, speed, "motion-domain"
    accelerated = speed + thrust * dt
    damped = accelerated * max(0.0, 1.0 - drag * dt)
    bounded = min(damped, max_speed)
    if bounded > max_speed:
        return False, bounded, "speed-cap"
    assert 0.0 <= bounded <= max_speed
    return True, round(bounded, 3), "inertial-ship"
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.
